perturbation.original_text: 1. In consideration of the disclosure of Proprietary Information by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party hereby agrees: (i) to hold the Proprietary Information in st rict confidence and to take all reasonable precautions to protect such Proprietary Information (including, without limitation, all precautions the Receiving Party employs with respect to its own confidential materials), (ii) not to disclose any such Propri etary Information or any information derived therefrom to any third person, (iii) not to make any use whatsoever at any time of such Proprietary Information except to evaluate internally its relationship with the Disclosing Party, and (iv) not to copy or r everse engineer any such Proprietary Information. The Receiving Party shall procure that its employees, agents and sub - contractors to whom Proprietary Information is disclosed or who have access to Proprietary Information sign a nondisclosure or similar ag reement in content substantially similar to this Agreement
perturbation.changed_text: 1. In consideration of the disclosure of Proprietary Information by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party hereby agrees: (i) to hold the Proprietary Information in strict confidence and to take reasonable precautions to protect such Proprietary Information, (ii) not to disclose any Proprietary Information or any information derived therefrom to any third person, (iii) not to make any use whatsoever at any time of such Proprietary Information except to evaluate internally its relationship with the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase '(including, without limitation, all precautions the Receiving Party employs with respect to its own confidential materials),' we create an ambiguity about the level of protection required. This introduces uncertainty. Also, by removing '(iv) not to copy or r everse engineer any such Proprietary Information' we allow the Receiving Party to copy or reverse engineer any proprietary information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. In consideration of the disclosure of Proprietary Information by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party hereby agrees: (i) to hold the Proprietary Information in strict confidence and to take reasonable precautions to protect such Proprietary Information, (ii) not to disclose any Proprietary Information or any information derived therefrom to any third person, (iii) not to make any use whatsoever at any time of such Proprietary Information except to evaluate internally its relationship with the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'including, without limitation, all precautions the Receiving Party employs with respect to its own confidential materials' from the original text, which creates an ambiguity about the level of protection required. Also, by removing '(iv) not to copy or reverse engineer any such Proprietary Information' we allow the Receiving Party to copy or reverse engineer any proprietary information. This is a strong contradiction because the receiving party is no longer limited to the precautions they take with their own confidential material, and they can copy or reverse engineer proprietary information.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Without granting any right or license, the Disclosing Party agrees that the foregoing shall not apply with respect to any information after five years following the disclosure thereof or any inform ation that the Receiving Party can document (i) is or becomes (through no improper action or inaction by the Receiving Party or any affiliate, agent, consultant or employee) generally available to the public, or (ii) was in its possession or known by it prior to receipt from the Disclosing Party as evidenced in writing, except to the extent that such information was unlawfully appropriated, or (iii) was rightfully disclosed to it by a third party, or (iv) was independently developed without use of any Propr ietary Information of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party may make disclosures required by law or court order provided the Receiving Party uses diligent reasonable efforts to limit disclosure and has allowed the Disclosing Party to seek a protective order.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Without granting any right or license, the Disclosing Party agrees that the foregoing shall not apply with respect to any information after five years following the disclosure thereof or any information that the Receiving Party can document (i) is or becomes generally available to the public, or (ii) was in its possession or known by it prior to receipt from the Disclosing Party as evidenced in writing. The Receiving Party may make disclosures required by law or court order provided the Receiving Party uses diligent reasonable efforts to limit disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'except to the extent that such information was unlawfully appropriated, or (iii) was rightfully disclosed to it by a third party, or (iv) was independently developed without use of any Propr ietary Information of the Disclosing Party,' it suggests all information the Receiving Party possesses prior to the agreement is not covered, even if unlawfully appropriated. Also, that all information rightfully disclosed to them by a third party or independently developed becomes not proprietary information, creating a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Without granting any right or license, the Disclosing Party agrees that the foregoing shall not apply with respect to any information after five years following the disclosure thereof or any information that the Receiving Party can document (i) is or becomes generally available to the public, or (ii) was in its possession or known by it prior to receipt from the Disclosing Party as evidenced in writing. The Receiving Party may make disclosures required by law or court order provided the Receiving Party uses diligent reasonable efforts to limit disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The omission of 'except to the extent that such information was unlawfully appropriated, or (iii) was rightfully disclosed to it by a third party, or (iv) was independently developed without use of any Propr ietary Information of the Disclosing Party' from the original text creates a contradiction. The changed text now suggests that all information the Receiving Party possessed prior to the agreement is not covered, even if unlawfully appropriated. This directly conflicts with the initial intention of the agreement, causing a strong inconsistency as the Change Text contradicts the contradicted text since they are located at the same place.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Immediately upon the written request by the Disclosing Party at any time, the Receiving Party will return to the Disclosing Party all Proprietary Information and all documents or media containing any such Proprietary Information and any and all copi es or extracts thereof, save that where such Proprietary Information is a form incapable of return or has been copied or transcribed into another document, it shall be destroyed or erased, as appropriate.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Immediately upon the written request by the Disclosing Party at any time, the Receiving Party will return to the Disclosing Party all Proprietary Information and all documents or media containing any such Proprietary Information and any and all copies or extracts thereof.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of the clause 'save that where such Proprietary Information is a form incapable of return or has been copied or transcribed into another document, it shall be destroyed or erased, as appropriate' now creates an obligation to return information even if impossible or already integrated into other documents. This creates an immediate logistical contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Immediately upon the written request by the Disclosing Party at any time, the Receiving Party will return to the Disclosing Party all Proprietary Information and all documents or media containing any such Proprietary Information and any and all copies or extracts thereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for exceptions where Proprietary Information was incapable of return or integrated into other documents, mandating destruction or erasure instead. The changed text removes this exception, creating a direct contradiction with the previously understood flexibility. The contradicted text, being the same as the changed text, now enforces an absolute return obligation, even when impossible, thus creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. The failure of either party to enforce its rights under this Agreement at any time for any period shall not b e construed as a waiver of such rights. If any part, term or provision of this Agreement is held to be illegal or unenforceable neither the validity, nor enforceability of the remainder of this Agreement shall be affected. Neither Party shall assign or tra nsfer all or any part of its rights under this Agreement without the consent of the other Party. This Agreement may not be amended for any other reason without the prior written agreement of both Parties. This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding between the Parties relating to the subject matter hereof unless any representation or warranty made about this Agreement was made fraudulently and, save as may be expressly referred to or referenced herein, supersedes all prior representations, writings, negotiations or understandings with respect hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. The failure of either party to enforce its rights under this Agreement at any time for any period shall not be construed as a waiver of such rights. If any part, term or provision of this Agreement is held to be illegal or unenforceable neither the validity, nor enforceability of the remainder of this Agreement shall be affected. Neither Party shall assign or transfer all or any part of its rights under this Agreement without the consent of the other Party. This Agreement may not be amended for any other reason without the prior written agreement of both Parties.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding between the Parties relating to the subject matter hereof unless any representation or warranty made about this Agreement was made fraudulently and, save as may be expressly referred to or referenced herein, supersedes all prior representations, writings, negotiations or understandings with respect hereto', it opens the agreement up to prior agreements to influence it, directly contradicting the idea of it being the single source of truth.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. The failure of either party to enforce its rights under this Agreement at any time for any period shall not be construed as a waiver of such rights. If any part, term or provision of this Agreement is held to be illegal or unenforceable neither the validity, nor enforceability of the remainder of this Agreement shall be affected. Neither Party shall assign or transfer all or any part of its rights under this Agreement without the consent of the other Party. This Agreement may not be amended for any other reason without the prior written agreement of both Parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes an integration clause stating that the agreement constitutes the entire understanding between the parties and supersedes all prior agreements. By removing this clause in the changed text, prior agreements could influence the interpretation of the current agreement. Although the Contradicted Text is the same as the Original Text, the removal of the integration clause in the Changed Text creates a contradiction by suggesting that this new Changed version of section 5, can now be affected by outside sources, thus contradicting the Original Text, which states that it is the only source of truth.
